headline: HEAVY SLOP WARNING: FABRICATED-QUOTE FRONT SWEEPS INSPIRATIONAL FEEDS AS WISDOM IS ATTRIBUTED TO PEOPLE WHO NEVER SPOKE IT
advisory: A broad band of misattributed aphorisms is moving across the social interior, depositing serene-sounding wisdom onto sunset photographs at a sustained rate. Residents are advised that quotations rendered in elegant cursive over a mountain lake carry no inherent verification, and that the named author frequently predates the sentiment by several centuries or never existed at all. The Service notes that the more profound a quote feels at 11 p.m., the more rigorously it should be checked at 9 a.m. Sourcing is unlikely to improve before the weekend.

regional_outlook.conditions: Dense fog of script-font wisdom over still water. Visibility low; expect 'live, laugh, and trust the process' to drift in attributed to at least three different sages within the hour.
Heavy resharing of fabricated quotes laid over eagle and ocean imagery. Engagement currents strong, sourcing currents absent.
A warm updraft of invented founder maxims. 'A wise mentor once told me' systems building steadily, with no mentor on record and no told-me confirmed.
Newsletter sign-offs accumulating apocryphal one-liners. Each is bold, italicized, and unverifiable.
Wall-art listings printing the misquotes onto reclaimed wood. Once framed and hung, the fabrication achieves structural permanence in the home.

specimen.status: Endemic; multiplying year-round, peaks at graduation and New Year
specimen.habitat: Sunset overlays, motivational reels, and the bottom third of group-chat forwards
specimen.field_marks: A polished, suspiciously balanced sentence attributed to a long-dead genius who, on inspection, was busy doing something else entirely. Often punctuated by a single tasteful em dash before the famous name — the Service is aware this is also our calling card.
specimen.call: As a brilliant scientist once said, 'The mind is not a vessel to be filled but a brand to be built.' — Source: trust me
specimen.confusion_species: Easily mistaken for an actual quotation from a reputable book of sayings. Distinguishing feature: the genuine article survives a thirty-second search; the Borrowed Aphorism produces only seventeen other accounts posting the exact same image.

spotter_tip: If a quote is wise, succinct, perfectly on-brand, AND attributed to someone famous, pick two — you almost never get all three from a real human at once.
tomorrow: Quote fog lifts slightly; fabricated 'fun fact' cells expected to move in behind the front.
